input.background: An important historical case-control study completed by Doll and Hill found that 647 of the 649 lung cancer cases were smokers. In contrast, 622 of 649 controls smoked.
input.question: Calculate the odds ratio (OR) associated with smoking.

output.answer: $$\widehat{OR} = \frac{647 \cdot 27}{622 \cdot 2} = 14.04$$ This suggests that the smokers had 14 times the risk of nonsmokers.
